methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 13 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Alpha School is a Series A AI & Software company founded by MacKenzie Price and Andrew Price. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 13 same-sector peers: 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 private firms including Palantir, Waymo, Figure AI, Applied Intuition, and Skild AI.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. A narrow peer set filtered by stage (Series A), timing (adjacent rounds), and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold required for statistical confidence. While 13 companies operate within the AI & Software sector, none satisfy the combined stage and tier constraints simultaneously. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias and obscure meaningful differences in business model, maturity, and market positioning across the broader AI & Software landscape.

Without a sufficiently sized comparable set meeting stage and tier criteria, this valuation cannot be triangulated to a defensible range using market multiples. Alternative methods—including revenue or ARR multiples, DCF analysis, or venture capital method benchmarking—would require additional data on Alpha School's financial performance, growth trajectory, and capital efficiency metrics. The primary uncertainty is the scarcity of early-stage AI & Software comparables with publicly available valuation data, limiting the power of peer-based approaches at the Series A stage.
